title: Limit status rendering has no direct regression coverage

evidence.quote: Picks the highest-pressure token in the window so the mini-bar surfaces the tightest constraint.
aria-valuenow must stay within [valuemin, valuemax] to be a valid ARIA progressbar
Surface the failure explicitly to match what the Limits tab already shows.

reasoning: The pool-header group has focused tests for deviation, oracle price, rebalance status, and uptime, but no direct test for LimitStatusValue. That leaves several user-visible contracts unprotected: selecting the highest-pressure L0/L1 token, formatting netflow/limit pairs, capping breached progress bars while preserving over-limit aria text, rendering virtual pools as neutral, and rendering query failures as an explicit amber state. These are the same kinds of formatting and degraded-mode behaviors the sibling files test, so a small regression in this cell could ship without any targeted failure.
recommendation: Add a `limit-status-value.test.tsx` beside the sibling tests covering at least normal L0/L1 rendering, over-limit capped aria state, virtual pool dash, empty trading limits dash, and `hasError` rendering.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included test set contains `deviation-cell.test.tsx`, `oracle-price-value.test.tsx`, `rebalance-status-value.test.tsx`, and `uptime-value.test.tsx`; there is no corresponding LimitStatusValue test despite this file owning independent formatting and degraded-mode logic.
suggestedRegressionTest: Render `LimitStatusValue` with two trading-limit rows where one row has higher pressure, assert the displayed pair comes from that row and the progressbar width/aria text are correct; separately assert `hasError` returns `Query failed` and virtual/empty inputs return the neutral dash.
minimumFixScope: Add targeted Vitest coverage for `ui-dashboard/src/components/pool-header/limit-status-value.tsx`; no production code change is required unless the new tests expose behavior drift.

history.note: Fixed by adding direct LimitStatusValue regressions for highest-pressure display, over-limit aria state, error, virtual, and empty states.
